detail: cause.refdes='R49' is neither source nor decoupling cap of rail 'VIN' (expected_dead_rails=['VIN'])

original_draft.source_quote: No power at all — no LEDs, no rail activity after connecting wall or battery power. Likely cause: Blown input fuse F1 on the main power-input path, or no voltage reaching sense resistor R49. Probe R49 for input voltage with DMM; if absent, check F1 for continuity in diode-mode/resistance.

evidence.reasoning: Quote literally names R49; R49 is in the FUNCTIONAL CANDIDATE MAP and exists in the graph.

evidence.source_quote_substring: no voltage reaching sense resistor R49
evidence.reasoning: If no voltage passes through R49, the resistor or its joint is open.
R49 sits on the input path before VIN distribution; an open R49 starves all downstream rails consistent with the no-rail-activity symptom.

original_draft.reasoning_summary: Open input sense resistor R49 prevents VIN from reaching downstream regulators; symptomatic of completely dead board.

detail: cause.refdes='U9' is neither source nor decoupling cap of rail '+1V2' (expected_dead_rails=['+1V2'])

original_draft.source_quote: Internal display dark or no video; USB ports also dead. External HDMI may or may not work. Likely cause: 1.2 V supply rail dead — both the TUSB8041 USB hub (U9) and the SN65DSI86 eDP bridge (U10) require 1.2 V to operate.

evidence.reasoning: U9 named literally; FUNCTIONAL MAP confirms U9 maps to graph U9.
U9 die-short pulls the 1.2V rail down — the rule explicitly lists 'TUSB8041 die damage shorting VCC_1V2 to GND' as a cause.
U9 is a load on +1V2 (graph confirms its decoupling on +1V2 via C37 region); a shorted U9 collapses +1V2.
With +1V2 collapsed, U10 (also 1.2V load) dies along with U9; USB and display outputs go down.

original_draft.reasoning_summary: TUSB8041 (U9) die short collapses the 1.2V rail, taking U10 down with it; both display and USB ports go dead.

original_draft.source_quote: Internal display dark or no video; USB ports also dead. Additionally, the display needs 3V3_PWR_AUX and USB_PWR (5 V) switched on by the System Controller. Probe C37 for 1.2 V first; if absent, diode-mode on nearby decoupling caps to detect short. Also confirm 3V3_PWR_AUX and 5 V with DMM.

evidence.reasoning: Failure of the 3V3_PWR_AUX regulator (U27 per graph as source_refdes) is one cause of the display being dark.

original_draft.reasoning_summary: U27 is the source_refdes of 3V3_PWR_AUX in the graph; if dead, 3V3_PWR_AUX collapses and the display loses its auxiliary supply. Note: U27 isn't named in the dump by refdes, so confidence is low — included only because cause.refdes evidence cannot literally name U27, this scenario will likely be filtered.
